title: MCP preflight cache
description: Per-server preflight state for the `systematic-debugging` foundation skill. Lives at `.vinta-ai-workflows/cache.yaml` in the target project (gitignored — never committed; entries reflect the current developer machine, not project-wide truth). Read + written by the rendered `systematic-debugging` SKILL.md and the `refresh-mcp-cache` CLI command. No TTL — entries stay valid until something fails. A failed MCP call mid-debug-session marks the offending server `dirty`, forcing the next debug run to re-preflight that server (and only that server).

schema_version.description: Schema major version. Bumped only on breaking changes.

mcp_preflight.description: Map of MCP server identifier (matching an entry in `skills.systematic-debugging.observability_mcp_servers` of `.vinta-ai-workflows.yaml`) to its last-known preflight state.

status.description: `ok` skips preflight on subsequent runs. Any other value forces a fresh preflight on the next debug run AND surfaces the entry to the user as needing attention.

verified_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp the entry was last written. For `ok` entries, the time the server was last reachable + invocable. For non-ok entries, the time the failure was observed.

tools_count.description: Number of tools the server exposed when last preflighted with `status: ok`. Sanity signal — a sudden drop to zero on a previously-ok server is suspicious.

error_message.description: Verbatim error message captured when `status` is non-ok. Quoted to the user on the next debug run so they can see what failed without re-triggering the call.

marked_dirty_during.description: Optional pointer to the debug session that flipped this entry to `dirty` (free-form — phase id, plan id, timestamp, whatever the caller passed). Helps trace which run discovered the failure.
